summary: P0 Scalability Refactor Testing Complete. All 18 backend tests passed. Frontend loads correctly for all 5 categories. The primary_category field was successfully added to all 13144 matches with a MongoDB index for O(1) queries.

issues: Route is /paper/:id (singular) not /papers/:id - this is by design, not a bug

backend: All 13144 matches have primary_category field (backfill complete)
primary_category values are valid category IDs
Index on primary_category exists in MongoDB
All 5 categories return consistent data (50 papers each)
GET /api/admin/status with X-Admin-Token returns scheduler status
GET /api/status returns system status with 250 papers and 10000+ matches
GET /api/papers/{id} returns paper detail with matches
Match counts per category sum to total completed matches
frontend: Homepage loads with Robotics leaderboard (50 papers, 2148 comparisons)
Category switching works: Robotics, Distributed Computing, Economics, Computational Physics, Biomolecules
Period filters visible: Most Recent, This Week, This Month, All Time
Paper detail page loads via /paper/:id route
Paper detail shows: title, authors, abstract, AI Impact Assessment
Navigation bar shows: Leaderboard, Model Analysis, Methodology, Admin, Sign in

seed_data_creation: None - tested with existing production data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 verified the P0 scalability refactor. Key changes tested: (1) All 13144 matches now have primary_category field denormalized from paper's primary category, (2) MongoDB index on primary_category exists for O(1) queries, (3) Leaderboard API uses indexed queries for category filtering, (4) All 5 categories (cs.RO, cs.DC, econ.GN, physics.comp-ph, q-bio.BM) work correctly with 50 papers each and 2000+ matches. The refactor replaces O(N×M) full collection scans with indexed queries.
